period_focus: 2019 methodology baseline, 2024 outcome/budget lead, 2026 program identity
evidence_level: B+ mixed outcome, C budget recovery until official finance join
short_verdict: A mixed-success case: 2024 visitor and global-content signals are strong, but direct recovery and visitor/economic-effect methodology require verification before budget-efficiency or waste conclusions.
why_it_matters: It prevents overclaiming failure when visitor and global-program signals are strong.
It prevents overclaiming success when direct revenue is low and economic-effect methodology remains unverified.
It shows why budget analysis needs paid/free split, local-capture data and visitor-counting methodology.
It creates a model for cultural-heritage festivals where public value may matter, but must still be evidenced.

stakeholders.contractor_causality_status: not supported from current public evidence

causal_chain.evidence: Yonhap reported 1.48 million visitors over 10 days, up 68% from the previous year, with 25 countries and 35 overseas performance groups.
Reporting said Andong identified tasks including strengthening representative programs, creative use of Korean mask dance, whole-city site preparation and foreign visitor convenience.

KTO and Korea Spirit Culture Foundation pages describe a 2026 festival centered on UNESCO-listed Korean mask dance, international mask-dance performances, contests, parades, citizen participation, night events and Hahoe Seonyu Julbulnori.
A fiscal-analysis article reports 2024 execution of 1.969B KRW, admission/own revenue of 104.08M KRW, 5.3% recovery and 18,925 paid visitors.
2019 reporting recorded economic-effect and visitor-count inflation criticism, double-count risk, and attempts to estimate valid visitors using stay-time survey methods.

not_supported_claims: The festival wasted budget solely because direct recovery was low.
The festival was budget efficient solely because 1.48 million people visited.
A contractor or subcontractor caused the low direct recovery.
next_evidence_needed: Local Finance 365 cost-accounting row for 2024 and 2025
Official evaluation report tables and visitor-count method
Paid/free program split and ticket revenue category
Local merchant spend, overnight stay, foreign visitor convenience and satisfaction data
Contract/RFP scope if outsourcing responsibility is later investigated

sources.title: Yonhap 2024 visitor/outcome report
sources.supports: 1.48M visitors, 68% increase, overseas groups, improvement tasks

sources.title: KTO Visit Korea festival page
sources.supports: 2026 period, operator, free/some paid pricing, program identity and food pricing

sources.title: Maeil 2019 evaluation report coverage
sources.supports: self-reliance improvement and visitor-count double-count caveat
